channel.title: WiFi.Report Blog - WiFi Security, Speed & Privacy Guides
channel.description: Expert guides on WiFi security, network optimization, speed testing, privacy protection, and troubleshooting. Free advice from WiFi.Report.

item.title: WiFi 7 (802.11be) Guide 2026: Speed, Devices & Should You Upgrade?
item.description: WiFi 7 delivers up to 46 Gbps with Multi-Link Operation. Find out what it means for your home network, which devices support it, and if upgrading makes sense in 2026.

item.title: Best Router Security Settings 2026: Complete Setup Guide
item.description: Configure the best security settings on your router right now. WPA3 encryption, firewall, guest network, firmware updates, and more with step-by-step instructions.

item.title: Ultimate WiFi Security Checklist 2026: 20 Steps to a Secure Network
item.description: 20 actionable steps to lock down your home WiFi in 2026. Enable WPA3, fix DNS leaks, block intruders, and protect every device on your network.

item.title: How to See Who Is Connected to Your WiFi (Every Device)
item.description: Step-by-step guide to check every device on your WiFi right now — from any phone, laptop, or your router admin panel. Find and block unknown users.

item.title: VPN Travel Hacks: How to Find Cheap Flights and Hotels in 2026
item.description: How a VPN can help you find cheaper flights and hotels by changing your apparent location. Tested tips for saving money on travel bookings.

item.title: WiFi 6 vs WiFi 6E: What's the Real Difference?
item.description: WiFi 6E adds the 6 GHz band to WiFi 6. Find out what that means for your home network, which devices support it, and whether it's worth upgrading.

item.title: WPA3 vs WPA2: Which WiFi Security Protocol Should You Use?
item.description: WPA3 vs WPA2 explained: how they differ, which provides better security, and how to enable WPA3 on your router for maximum WiFi protection.

item.title: How to Map WiFi Dead Zones in Your Home
item.description: How to find and fix WiFi dead zones in your home using free tools and smart router placement strategies.

item.title: Why Is My WiFi Strong But Internet Slow? (Solved)
item.description: Full WiFi bars but slow internet? Here are 11 reasons why your WiFi signal can be strong while your internet speed suffers, and how to fix each one.

item.title: Why Free Public VPNs Are Dangerous (And What to Use Instead)
item.description: Free VPNs often sell your data, inject ads, or are outright malware. Here's why you should avoid them and what reputable paid alternatives to use instead.

item.title: How to Tell if Your WiFi Has Been Hacked (7 Warning Signs)
item.description: 7 clear warning signs your WiFi has been compromised, how to check for unauthorised devices, and how to secure your router immediately.

item.title: WebRTC vs DNS Leaks: Which One Exposes Your Identity?
item.description: WebRTC leaks and DNS leaks can both expose your real IP address even when using a VPN. Understand the difference and how to test for both.

item.title: Best WiFi Channel for Faster Speeds: 2.4 GHz vs 5 GHz Guide
item.description: Which WiFi channel should you use? How to find the least congested channel for 2.4 GHz and 5 GHz to maximise your network speed.

item.title: Mesh WiFi vs Traditional Routers: Which Is Better for You?
item.description: Mesh WiFi systems vs a single router with extenders: performance comparison, cost analysis, and which setup works best for different home sizes.

item.title: Incognito Mode Is a Lie: What Your ISP Can Still See
item.description: Incognito mode hides your browsing from others on your device but not from your ISP or network. Here's what it does and doesn't protect.
